core_loop: wrapper.fight() outer loop drives wrapper.engagement() per beat: Approach(close/stop-hit)→AwaitTempo(reopen)→Exchange(commit depth 2-5, mode select, sigma resolution via core.roll_net/degree)→Bind/Riposte/HitLanded/Contact→Felled/Separation. scene_combat_design_v1.md wraps N-vs-M bouts, emitting one scene.combat_resolved Key per scene. Resolution: pool=max(5,History+6), continuous sigma-leverage engine, bilateral-Ob wounds (ED-1041).

decision_points.where: pre-fight loadout: weapon + tradition/ability equip + armor tier
decision_points.options: 53-weapon roster with per-element primitives, half-sword/grippable affordances, equippable tradition modulators
decision_points.note: rich substrate but amortized over a whole fight/campaign, not per-exchange

decision_points.where: mid-exchange: commit depth, mode select, grip-choke, retreat-vs-press
decision_points.options: physically these vary continuously (grip continuum, thrust-protection, choke counterbalance)
decision_points.note: engine-resolved by disposition/physics/RNG in wrapper.engagement(A,B,first,cfg,rng) — no player-decision parameter exists anywhere in the per-beat loop or state_graph.py
declared-only in module_contracts.yaml; no code path from this Key into engagement()'s actual decision surfaces

north_star.collision_feed: Near-nothing currently escapes the fight: scene.combat_resolved's schema carries only scene_id/outcome/participants/casualties/wounds_inflicted (a health-bar-shaped payload), and its declared consumers (npc_behavior/faction_layer/articulation) don't list it in their own consumes: contracts — a double bottleneck (thin payload, and even that isn't read downstream).
north_star.verdict: The subsystem is an unusually rigorous physics simulation but resolves almost entirely autonomously — real per-exchange player agency and narrative payload are both thin, which works against a North Star built on myriad player decisions and collision-fed emergent narrative.

nq.observation: Spear/polearm 84-96% dominance is a live non-dominance violation under active repair (R2 merged, R3 ratified but unexecuted); voulge/bardiche 81-88% retention still flagged borderline (JD-1), unresolved.
Dramatic-legibility fails from this subsystem's state alone: Health/Wounds numbers carry no political stake, intent, or season-clock hook.

The R1/R3 plans show unusually strong scale-transition discipline — layer purity L0-L3, byte-identical defaults, ablation gates, forced dependency ordering — a model of engineering-smooth practice worth citing as a positive pattern for other lanes.
nq.cite: plan_r1_RATIFIED.md Layer discipline section; consolidation_v1.md §4 dependency spine

nq.observation: Depth is straining simplicity: 5-tuple mode-select returns, per-element percussion, a grip-reach oscillator fix (JD-9) — the core rule is no longer restatable after one reading, a legitimate elegance cost of chasing biomechanical realism.

threadwork_junctures.evidence: ED-911 (open, P1), KNOWN-TRACKED. New scope this pass: both plan_r1_RATIFIED.md (2026-07-02) and consolidation_v1.md (2026-07-04), authored AFTER ED-911 opened, contain zero Thread/Coherence/Leap mentions and no thread-related increment in the U0-U9 sequence — two consecutive major engineering passes have hardened the substrate's layering without scheduling the gap closed, compounding retrofit cost.

threadwork_junctures.juncture: Coherence-state gating legibility or commit depth mid-exchange
threadwork_junctures.evidence: A mid-fight Leap would plausibly be a huge legibility tell, or a Coherence-spend could be a real commit-depth resource decision; no hook exists in systems.legibility or the commit-depth machinery in any read document.

03_threadwork_surface.md §2 confirms the superseded combat_v30.md §10 had Leap-during-combat text that never migrated to combat_engine_v1 — the gap is a regression from a prior design, not an original absence.

legibility.note: Whose position is at risk is only partially answerable (Health/Wounds numerically ranks proximity to Felled, no political stake named); what each actor wants and what happens if no one acts next season are both unanswerable from this subsystem's state alone — combat resolves synchronously within a scene with no goal field or season-clock hook, and the Echo channel that would carry consequence upward is itself unconsumed on the receiving side.

degenerate_candidates: Spear/polearm 84-96% dominance pre-R2, still borderline post-R2 for voulge/bardiche (81-88%, JD-1 open) — plan_r1_RATIFIED.md D2 retention table
Retreat-as-default (U6) not yet implemented — until it lands, press-vs-retreat is a physics-derived tempo outcome, not a real player-held choice axis — consolidation_v1.md §4 U6 row (PENDING)

edges.other: faction (via Domain Echo, Sufficient Scope gate)

directions.top-down: Omega non-dominance is actively if incompletely served by the degrade-under-circumstance program; P-14 inseparability is not served at all — zero thread hooks.
directions.bottom-up: The weapon-physics/geometry substrate is unusually rigorous and provenance-honest ([ASSERTED]/[FIAT]/[SIM-CALIBRATE] labeling), a model for other lanes.
directions.lateral: Social contest and mass battle both have live threadwork junctures; personal combat is the one scale-sibling with none — a structural asymmetry, not a scale-specific reason.
directions.diagonal: The Domain Echo channel meant to carry combat outcomes upward is itself general (not combat-authored) and unconsumed on the receiving side — a cross-scale double gap.
directions.forwards: R3's U0-U9/T-P2/T5 sequence is internally well-ordered but reserves no slot for ED-911 (threadwork) or a player-input surface — both need net-new sequence entries.
directions.backwards: combat_v30.md §10 (superseded) had Leap-in-combat text that never migrated to combat_engine_v1, confirming the thread gap is a regression, not an original absence.

findings.claim: wrapper.engagement() and the entire per-beat state graph have no player-decision parameter or input hook; commit depth, mode selection, grip-choke, and retreat-vs-press are all engine/RNG-resolved, so personal combat is a build-time-loadout-plus-autoresolve system rather than a per-exchange decision space.
findings.cite: designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/wrapper.py engagement() signature (A,B,first,cfg,rng); state_graph.py STATES/INJECTION_POINTS (all engine-internal); references/module_contracts.yaml personal_combat consumes: scene.combat_strike declared but unwired

findings.claim: ED-911 (no threadwork in combat_engine_v1) is KNOWN, but two consecutive major post-opening engineering passes (R2, R3) hardened the substrate's layering without ever scheduling it, so the retrofit cost is compounding rather than static.
findings.cite: designs/audit/2026-07-02-scene-combat-closing-distance-redesign/plan_r1_RATIFIED.md (full text, zero Thread mentions); designs/audit/2026-07-04-weapon-morphology-granularity/consolidation_v1.md §4 U0-U9 (no thread increment)

findings.claim: scene.combat_resolved's Key schema carries only scene_id/outcome/participants/casualties/wounds_inflicted (no scars/tradition-identity/narrative field), and its own declared consumers omit it from their consumes: contracts — narrative payload is both thin and unconsumed.
findings.cite: designs/scene/scene_combat_v1/scene_combat_design_v1.md §Roll-up into the scene.combat_resolved Key; references/module_contracts.yaml personal_combat gap_notes CONSUMER WIRING (A4)

findings.claim: The dramatic-legibility test fails from this subsystem's state alone — no goal/intent field, no political-stake naming, no season-clock hook; the fight resolves as a synchronous numeric event.

Tradition/ability design deliberately normalizes every tradition to an equal affinity budget for anti-dominance, which narrows build-time differentiation exactly where the corpus wants meaningful choice.
findings.cite: designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/tradition_decomposition_v1.md (WS-4 dissolution: equal affinity budget, 6.8pp win-rate spread measured)

findings.claim: Successive biomechanical-fidelity passes (5-tuple mode-select return, per-element percussion, grip-reach oscillator fix) are eroding Q-elegant's 'restatable after one reading' bar even though each individual increment is well-gated.
